description: AgentHub is a marketplace and platform for discovering, deploying, and monetizing AI agents.

listing_process.overview: AgentHub allows developers to list AI agents for others to discover, use, and integrate.

steps.details: Sign up at agenthub.dev with a developer account. Verification may be required.
Ensure the agent is containerized (Docker) or packaged as a runnable service. Agents typically need an API endpoint or a defined interface.
Provide name, description, category, tags, pricing model (free, usage-based, subscription), and documentation.
Upload the agent configuration via the developer dashboard. AgentHub reviews submissions for quality, security, and compliance with platform guidelines.
Upon approval, the agent is listed on the marketplace. Developers can track usage analytics and earnings.

technical: Docker container or serverless function deployable via AgentHub infrastructure
API interface compatible with AgentHub's integration standards
Security best practices (input validation, rate limiting, authentication)
quality_standards: Compliance with AgentHub terms of service

publishing_options.public: Listed in the marketplace for all users
publishing_options.private: Available only to specific teams or organizations
publishing_options.unlisted: Accessible via direct link but not shown in search results

monetization.pay_per_use: Charges based on API calls or usage tokens

review_process.duration: Typically 3-7 business days for initial review
review_process.appeal: Developers can appeal rejections with additional information

integration_guide.interface_requirements: Agents expose endpoints that accept JSON inputs and return JSON outputs
integration_guide.authentication: API key or OAuth-based authentication integrated with AgentHub's auth system
integration_guide.monitoring: Built-in logging and usage tracking provided by AgentHub

listing_process.sources_note: This research is based on publicly available information about AgentHub.dev as of January 2025. The actual process may have changed. For authoritative details, refer to the official AgentHub developer documentation at agenthub.dev.
